ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Tɩchɛ ban wa de a lɛ baar, ba vʊ̃n nɛɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a puo sʊɔ,
translated: The dog chases the goat, it runs very fast,

ref: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
text: A yar mɩ yuo yuor, ka a Nãaŋmɩn nɩbɛ bamɩne na dɔ̃ɔ kpi, jɛɔ.
translated: I am happy to see you, and I greet Naaŋmɩn people who are here with us today.

ref: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
text: A bɩbir dɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ ɩ naa a Paanʊ̃kõo Lɔ̃ɔfʊ, ka a Yesu poturbo wa sʊʊr wʊ, “Nyɩnɛ lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ɩka sɩ chen tɩ chɔɔr a Gõl Bar nyã Difʊ bar ka sɩ tɩ di?”
translated: The man was mute, and when he saw Jesus, he asked him, “Will you cure my child, or should I take her to the traditional healer to be treated?”

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: ka a nɩbɛ piel sʊɔrɔ a Yesu ka wʊ yi a ba wɛr pɔ.
translated: He does not have to fear because Jesus has taken away his sin.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Tɩchɛ ka bamɩne 'yɛr, “Yɩ bar a sɩ kaara, Elaija na wa yɔɔ wʊ naa bɩɩ.”
translated: They said to him, “Today you are going to eat, Elijah has come to eat with you.”

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
